candidates.name: Matt Meyer
candidates.notes: DE Governor since Jan 21, 2025 (76th Gov); term to Jan 2029, next gubernatorial election 2028 (no 2028 filing yet). Former New Castle County Executive (2017-2025). Fast-accumulating HARD record of bills signed/EOs — consistently progressive, with one genuine cross-current on public safety. SIGNED the Medical Aid in Dying Act (HB140) his predecessor had vetoed; signed HB205 abortion/IVF/gender-affirming-care shield law; joined the Reproductive Freedom Alliance; PP Advocacy Fund of DE endorsed his 2024 run. Signed EO#11 protecting gender-affirming care (incl. minors) and EO#20 expanding the LGBTQ+ Commission; backs marriage-equality amendment. Signed a FY26 budget adding new top income-tax brackets. Signed anti-ICE bills (HB182 bars 287(g) agreements) and EO#9 (Office of Gun Violence Prevention). CROSS-CURRENT: signed pro-law-enforcement bills (SB38 retired police-animal care, SB112 academy naming) and HB123 (new felony for discharging firearms at homes/businesses/places of worship) — documented credit on public-justice.

profile.education: Brown University (BA, Political Science & Computer Science); University of Michigan Law School (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and former New Castle County executive; grew up in Wilmington, Delaware; became 76th governor in January 2025.

candidates.name: Kathy Jennings
candidates.notes: DE AG since 2019 (46th AG), reelected 2022, running 2026 for a third term (first declared candidate, Feb 2025). Abundant HARD evidence via multistate briefs/lawsuits — consistent progressive, no cross-party deviations. Pro-abortion: amicus briefs defending abortion access and mifepristone; runs a free abortion legal helpline. Pro-trans: sued HHS and Trump admin to protect gender-affirming care. Progressive prosecutor: reduced mandatory minimums, diversion over incarceration, supports risk-based (not cash) bail. Defended DE vote-by-mail/same-day-registration and sued over Trump voting-restriction EOs. Defends DE's assault-weapons/magazine and ghost-gun bans ('third win over the gun lobby'). Sued to block Trump's birthright-citizenship EO and immigration-enforcement funding conditions.

candidates.name: Chris Coons
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Delaware (2010-). Yale Divinity School graduate — one of only three senators with divinity degree. Ordained Presbyterian elder. Heritage Action 3%. NRA F. Despite theological education, votes consistently progressive on social issues. Bipartisan Senate Prayer Breakfast participant.

profile.education: Amherst College (BA Chemistry/Political Science), Yale Divinity School (MAR), Yale Law School (JD)
profile.background: Former New Castle County Executive and attorney at W.L. Gore & Associates. Has degrees in divinity and law from Yale. Won 2010 special election for Biden's former Senate seat.
profile.office: 218 Russell Senate Office Building

claims.text: Receives a 100% rating from the Planned Parenthood Action Fund and a 0% rating from the National Right to Life Committee, placing him squarely within the abortion-advocacy endorsement and funding network.

Voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (2022), codifying federal recognition of same-sex unions and requiring states to honor such marriages — explicitly rejecting the one-man-one-woman definition of marriage.

Sponsored provisions expanding firearm purchase disqualifications and requiring local law enforcement notification when prohibited persons attempt to buy guns — supporting the gun-control infrastructure the rubric opposes.

candidates.name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Delaware (2025-). First woman and first African American to represent Delaware in Congress. Previously served in U.S. House (2017-2025). Heritage Action lifetime 3%. NRA F rating. Family attended Bright Hope Church for decades.

profile.education: Fairleigh Dickinson University (BA International Relations), University of Delaware (MA Urban Affairs/Public Policy)
profile.background: Former Delaware Secretary of Labor and U.S. House member. Served in cabinets of two Delaware governors. First woman and first African American to represent Delaware in Congress.
profile.office: 513 Hart Senate Office Building

claims.text: Pledged on entering the Senate to again vote to codify Roe v. Wade into federal law: 'We cannot let my Republican colleagues pass a nationwide abortion ban. I voted to codify Roe in the House. I will do it again in the Senate.' — rejecting personhood from conception.

Endorsed by and consistently aligned with Planned Parenthood; has opposed every legislative effort to restrict taxpayer funding of Planned Parenthood and abortion services, placing her inside the abortion-industry financial network.

Voted against the March 2025 Republican continuing-resolution bill and publicly rejected spending-restraint frameworks, positioning herself against balanced-budget efforts and opposing cuts to social-safety-net spending programs.

candidates.name: Sarah McBride
candidates.notes: First openly transgender member of Congress (DE-AL, elected Nov 2024); previously DE state senator 2021-2024. Running for reelection 2026. Uniformly progressive roll-call record in the 119th: NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 27, 1/23/2025); NAY on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, 1/14/2025); NAY on SAVE Act proof-of-citizenship voter registration (Roll 102, 4/10/2025); NO on Laken Riley Act (Roll 6, 1/7/2025). Heritage Action 0% (119th, session and lifetime). Equality Caucus co-chair and Equality Act (H.R. 15) reintroducer. As DE senator: co-sponsored the End-of-Life Options Act (assisted suicide, HB 140) and sponsored SB 2 permit-to-purchase; Giffords-endorsed, committed to an assault-weapons ban. One rubric-aligned vote: supported the Jan 2026 Venezuela war-powers resolution (House tied 215-215) to require congressional authorization. Notable tone deviation (not a vote): complied with the Capitol bathroom policy, calling the fight a distraction.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (ordained PCUSA elder; longtime member, Westminster Presbyterian Church, Wilmington)
profile.office: 1306 Longworth House Office Building

candidates.name: Eric Hansen
candidates.office: U.S. Senate Delaware (2026 R Candidate · businessman)
candidates.notes: Delaware businessman. R candidate for DE U.S. Senate 2026 vs Coons.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 DE U.S. Senate challenger. Archetype: establishment_r.

claims.text: Explicitly stated 'I, as a U.S. Senator, will not vote for a federal abortion ban, full stop'; supports only late-term abortion limits with exceptions for rape, incest, and life of the mother — rejecting personhood-from-conception and federal restrictions on abortion.

Backed the failed 2024 bipartisan border reform bill over a wall-construction or mass-deportation framework; characterized illegal immigration as a vetting problem requiring stricter legal screening rather than military enforcement at the border.
Proposes a national school choice program giving families the ability to direct education funds to the school of their choosing, expanding parental authority over children's schooling.

candidates.name: Kyle Evans Gay
candidates.notes: 27th Lieutenant Governor of Delaware (D), sworn in 2025-01-21. Replaces phantom record 'tonya-williams' (no such DE LG existed).

profile.background: Delaware State Senator, SD-5 (2021-2025); 27th Lieutenant Governor of Delaware, sworn in 2025-01-21.
